subject: OOM changes related to SO AAF Integration for SSL Certs
commit_message:
OOM changes related to SO AAF Integration for SSL Certs

This patch includes AAF SSL feature where SSL certs
can be optionally turned on with global variable
aaf.ssl.certs.enabled=true. This feature is turned
off by default

Issue-ID: SO-2453
Signed-off-by: Ramesh Parthasarathy(rp6768)<ramesh.parthasarathy@att.com>
Change-Id: Ic857f87bb4c8ec4863db2d153f963192db80e20e

Patch Set 3:

(8 comments)

Hello,
I'm not really happy with the jks, keyfile and trust.jks being directly in git.
Can you explain if there's a security breach or not on this point?

Question for information (as we're having a discussion around this): Is it possible to have both HTTP AND HTTPS ports running at the same time on a container or it's only one OR the other? The answer will change the way we create internal services

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

@Sylvain
Based on the feedback I got from Ramesh it's not possible to generate client certificate on startup (I still need to confirm this with Amy and AAF team). If this is true then we have 2 options:
1) Put in in container image,
2) Provide it to pod at deployment

Both options are equally insecure and both compromise the certificate but option #2 allows you to at least replace the certificate with your own one at the deployment without rebuilding the containers.

Nevertheless those file should be provided as secrets not as resources so I also vote -1 on this.

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

Please work with Krzysztof to address his concerns

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3:
> 
> (8 comments)
> 
> Hello,
> I'm not really happy with the jks, keyfile and trust.jks being directly in git.
> Can you explain if there's a security breach or not on this point?
> 
> Question for information (as we're having a discussion around this): Is it possible to have both HTTP AND HTTPS ports running at the same time on a container or it's only one OR the other? The answer will change the way we create internal services

Right now, SO supports either http or https (not both). Steve and/or Max can weigh-in on this.

Patch Set 3:

(7 comments)

> Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1
> 
> @Sylvain
> Based on the feedback I got from Ramesh it's not possible to generate client certificate on startup (I still need to confirm this with Amy and AAF team). If this is true then we have 2 options:
> 1) Put in in container image,
> 2) Provide it to pod at deployment
> 
> Both options are equally insecure and both compromise the certificate but option #2 allows you to at least replace the certificate with your own one at the deployment without rebuilding the containers.
> 
> Nevertheless those file should be provided as secrets not as resources so I also vote -1 on this.

Assuming that we are onboard with option 2), I'll go ahead & convert these jks file into secrets. I'll leave keyfile as a resource so that it can be used for AAF password encryption. Please let me know if you are ok with this.
